posts.text:
🚨 SOS – Urgent 
Cutting internet during war is a war crime.
Iranians have no sirens, no shelters, no warning.
A digital blackout leaves civilians blind when bombs fall.
That’s not security. That’s cruelty.
@elonmusk 
@realDonaldTrump

Video

Iranian Women’s Soccer Players Withdraw Asylum Request !

Would anyone flee a dictatorship… then suddenly go back? I know their hostage diplomacy playbook very well.

The Iranian media now announced that Mona Hamoudi, Zahra Sarbali, and Zahra Meshkinkar, two players and a staff member of Iran’s women’s national football team, have withdrawn their asylum request in Australia and are heading back to Iran.

Today, Iran’s police chief Ahmadreza Radan, openly declared that “security forces have their fingers on the trigger, ready to shoot protesters.”

That is why I called on the United States and democratic nations to remove him. And to you Prime Minister @sanchezcastejon, When an official signals a willingness to massacre protesters, the international community has a responsibility to act before thousands of innocent people are killed.

Video

Mr. President, Iran is not a nation of terror and hate. The Islamic Republic is.
Iran is a nation of poets, artists, scientists, and millions of brave women and men who long to live in dignity and freedom. Our country has been hijacked by barbaric regime.

posts.text:
Heartbreaking story.
An Iranian football player broke down while explaining how the Islamic Republic threatened and punished her simply for being a woman athlete in Iran.
Feminists teach women to use that signal when they're in danger. But when Iranian players flashed SOS signs after refusing to sing the regime's anthem in Australia, guess who rushed to help these women?
Feminist icons or President @realDonaldTrump?
Watch the story.
Thanks @marthamaccallum for telling @Shiva_amini_11’s story.

V

posts.text:
We Iranians outside the country hear your threats every day. You threaten our lives, our families, our mothers. You even sent killers to my house in New York City. But we are not afraid anymore.
Our only concern is the lives of Iranians inside the country who are facing massacre. They are our heroes, and we will continue to echo their voices. We will keep fighting until the day the Islamic Republic is gone.

We may be wounded, but we will never bow to our oppressors. 
💔✌️




Masih Alinejad 🏳️ (

Iranian authority openly threatening to kill its critics on national television.

I invite everyone to listen carefully to these threats broadcast on Iranian state TV.
“Confiscating property is nothing… we will make your mothers mourn you.”

For Iranian activists, journalists, and opposition figures, this is not rhetoric. It is a real and constant threat.

posts.text:
This is what “No to Hijab Day” actually looks like.

Same Iranian women.
Same players.

The only difference is freedom.

I hope FIFA and the Olympic Committee look carefully at this photo and understand how the Islamic Republic uses sports to normalize an apartheid regime.
#Iran
